title: Does it matter if the soul in the Eva is the mother
display_title: Does it matter if the soul in the Eva is the mother
wikitext:
[]: Does it matter if the soul in the Eva is the mother?             Quote Post by CyberXIII » Wed Nov 04, 2009 6:00 pm I know Anno wanted to use the mother theme throughout Eva, but I was wondering about this. The soul in the Eva is there so the pilot can synchronize with it. But does it have to be the actual mother? What if the pilot had been adopted and never knew their true parents? Or what if they had put the father in instead?

[]:             Quote Post by Otakon 08 Ikari » Wed Nov 04, 2009 6:25 pm That is an interesting question, does the soul HAVE to be the mother or could it be someone else who still cares for the pilot? I truly do not know..

[]:             Quote Post by Boredom » Wed Nov 04, 2009 6:30 pm I always wondered whose souls where in the MP Eva series.

[]:             Quote Post by Sammaeloo » Wed Nov 04, 2009 6:33 pm What if it was a child who thought he was raised by birds, and the soul was that of a parakeet.

[]:             Quote Post by Otakon 08 Ikari » Wed Nov 04, 2009 6:34 pm Boredom wrote: I always wondered whose souls where in the MP Eva series.

[]:             Quote Post by schismatics » Wed Nov 04, 2009 6:57 pm Otakon 08 Ikari wrote: Boredom wrote: I always wondered whose souls where in the MP Eva series.

[]:             Quote Post by thewayneiac » Wed Nov 04, 2009 7:24 pm No, the Kaworu Dummy Plugs have Kaworu's personality imprinted on them, not his soul. They probably used a variation of the same technology they used to imprint Naoko Akagi's personality on the Magi. The Rei Plugs have Rei's personality. The MP Evas still need an actual soul in the core to synch with the Dummy Plug.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Wed Nov 04, 2009 8:19 pm It's implied that the synchronization is partly due to the unconscious and instinctual connection a mother has for her child. It's that instinct that takes over when the Evas go berserk. If you lose the mother then you lose that connection so, yeah, I'd say it's essential.

[]:             Quote Post by Uriel Septim VII » Wed Nov 04, 2009 8:54 pm I wonder if any of the students noticed all of them had absent mothers.

[]:             Quote Post by Otakon 08 Ikari » Wed Nov 04, 2009 9:22 pm Uriel Septim VII wrote: I wonder if any of the students noticed all of them had absent mothers.

[]:             Quote Post by Teague » Wed Nov 04, 2009 9:28 pm Eva Yojimbo wrote: It's implied that the synchronization is partly due to the unconscious and instinctual connection a mother has for her child. It's that instinct that takes over when the Evas go berserk. If you lose the mother then you lose that connection so, yeah, I'd say it's essential.

[]:             Quote Post by Otakon 08 Ikari » Wed Nov 04, 2009 9:34 pm You speak a lot of good points, we need to continue this conversation further. I like the idea that if an enemy wanted you hurt so badly that they would allow you into the core and thus connection would be made.

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Wed Nov 04, 2009 10:33 pm Boredom wrote: I always wondered whose souls where in the MP Eva series.

[]:             Quote Post by Otakon 08 Ikari » Wed Nov 04, 2009 10:38 pm Joseph the PRPD wrote: Boredom wrote: I always wondered whose souls where in the MP Eva series.

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Wed Nov 04, 2009 10:50 pm Are you referring to Kaworu again?

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Wed Nov 04, 2009 11:29 pm Teague wrote: But there are other unconscious bonds of equal stength... Personally I think any powerful connection could tie a pilot to their EVA, A lover, a sibling, a life-long companion or close friend...

[]:             Quote Post by Uriel Septim VII » Wed Nov 04, 2009 11:44 pm Eva Yojimbo wrote: Teague wrote: But there are other unconscious bonds of equal stength... Personally I think any powerful connection could tie a pilot to their EVA, A lover, a sibling, a life-long companion or close friend...

[]:             Quote Post by SaltyJoe » Thu Nov 05, 2009 3:02 am What Teague wrote, but, since i think the bond between mother and child is the strongest natural bond imaginable, i would say the having a mom-in-the-can is the "optimal" choice. Teague wrote: I'd even hazard hate could work...

[]:             Quote Post by Desty_Nova » Thu Nov 05, 2009 3:58 am I think it's still open to debate. For example when Kaji is on the phone with gendo during the gaghiel(?) attack, Gendo says that Shinji is there as a spare/backup pilot. An odd thing to say when Asuka is presumably the only one able to pilot eva-02

[]:             Quote Post by Taekmkm » Thu Nov 05, 2009 4:40 am She has the highest synch ratio with unit 2, but they never said the units are solely encoded to the pilots. Note the aplently threats of switching Rei with Shinji, Asuka with Rei, dummy plug, etc. Shinji can still pilot Unit 2, just at a much lower capacity.
summary:
[]: Does it matter if the soul in the Eva is the mother?             Quote Post by CyberXIII » Wed Nov 04, 2009 6:00 pm I know Anno wanted to use the mother theme throughout Eva, but I was wondering about this. The soul in the Eva is there so the pilot can synchronize with it. But does it have to be the actual mother? What if the pilot had been adopted and never knew their true parents? Or what if they had put the father in instead?

[]: &n
